channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money? — 50 replies — 200.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money?

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-06-13 12:55 UTC

OP

>Tesla makes $3.9B/year, and the amount goes down year after year, yet has a 1.5T market cap
>SpaceX literally loses money and its claim to future profit is Grok, a cash-burning AI model that is losing to GPT and Claude
>Twitter is breakeven at best
What's going on here? Are white boomers just throwing money at him because they don't like seeing Indians, Chinese and Jews running shit in tech? This is the only explanation for the Elon Musk phenomenon I can make any sense of.

Early replies (first 3)

62361459
>>62361452
People rightly see him as a visionary for the future of humanity with the ability to actually do the stuf he foresees.

on the negative side much of what he envisions is distopian

and the first person to pioneer a technology is often NOT the person who gets rich off it

but you have to start somewhere, and he certainly does stuff other companies can't.

62361461
companies do not have to be profitable for people to speculate on them performing well in the future, and it helps when everyone said every company would go to zero but instead became some of the most valuable assets by marketcap in the world because they provided goods and services people wanted

62361471
>>62361459
>rightly
He hasn't even done anything, period.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62361989
>>62361983
Absofuckinglutely dude
I genuinely do admire Elon but not for the reason his fans do, his actual body of work- Fooling investors- is a talent level I've never seen

62361998
>>62361989
I think that's why he loves Saars so much. He just relates to them, scammer to scammer.

62362021
>>62361542
Warren Buffett: owns companies that provide goods and services people actually buy.
Elon Musk: tells retards he's going to Mars and he assumes this shit that's never going happen, is worth trillions in discounted future earnings. Meanwhile his actual IRL companies don't even make any money after being around for 20 years.

He is a scammer and you are his paypig. Congratulations.

62362038
>>62361499
>But it is now the future, and they are performing poorly, the BYD makes superior electric vehicles,
Yeah that's another thing. His EV company has been defeated by China. What happens when China starts a space company. Will his paypigs still keep bidding up his unprofitable companies for no reason, or will they become China fans instead?

62362068
>>62361478
the space industry was flailing on its last shitdick before spacex came into the picture. i hate him and his companies, but that much is true. he invigorated people and showed them that private space flight was not only possible, but that it could attract even government contracts. as for tesla, he put electric cars on the global market at a bigger scale than ever and shifted public attitude towards them. you have to understand the cultural context his companies came out of.

62362141
>>62361966
>Belle Delphine is technically a trillionaire too since she has an unlimited supply of bath water
now, this is analology

62362170
>>62361515
If you live outside of the US, you can see with your own eyes the meteoric rise of Chinese vehicles.

62362179
>>62361452
The greater fool game.
One day the bubble will not just pop it'll burst.

62362395
>>62361452
people don't want to buy shit that makes their lives marginally better

a better toaster or a nicer laptop or an AI chat tool is not meaningfully changing people's lives

you know what does? a dream for a future where we are on mars and doing cool space shit

62362428
>>62362395
>a dream for a future where we are on mars and doing cool space shit
That's literally nothing. I have a plan to get us to Andromeda galaxy. Give me $10T right now.

62362436
>>62362428
I mean, if you had big ass space craft and thousands of engineers and a plan, maybe yea you could get $10T. But you don't.

62362441
>>62362436
>I mean, if you had big ass space craft and thousands of engineers and a plan, maybe yea you could get $10T. But you don't.
He doesn't have enough of that stuff to get a man to mars ether, yet still he gets a trillion for owning the least profitable large cap tech stocks in America.

62362449
>>62362441
you can read the OP in my thread here: >>62362383
spaceX in their own documents aren't saying they believe in space exploration
that's just the story they are selling the the goys and retail investors

spaceX plans to make its money on building data centers, just like anthropic, oracle, openai, google, literally every other tech company right now
and again, to be clear, I agree with you this is stupid and this bubble will eventually pop. or maybe it won't. the dotcom bubble never really popped, the market just kept growing and software really was that valuable.

anyways. the stories Musk tells about space exploration are mainly marketing to get dumb people and X followers excited, he has already realized he can't deliver on that goal. the big picture for spaceX doesn't involve space exploration at all.

62362464
>>62362449
>the dotcom bubble never really popped
Get a load of this baggie.

62362537
>>62361452
Go back to rebbit, faggot.

62362557
>>62361478
>He
He doesn't do shit in his companies

62362583
He's ran a very good public relations campaign the last few decades. He made sure that his name was associated with his products. Tesla is synonymous with Elon Musk

62362589
>>62361452
Guy I'm stuck in e13 figure hell and people are making fun of me online. What do?

62362674
>>62362449
maybe the data center angle is just what they are selling to the institutional investors, and they really do believe in space exploration?

62362684
>>62361478
>robotics
he hasn't sold a single robot

62362688
>>62362068
he received massive subsidies the entire time in tesla
That's not his doing. Politicians wanted EVs no matter if he produced them or someone else did
They were already was creating a car before he joined Tesla

62362689
>>62361452
Of all the famous billionaires, Musk's companies are the only truly innovative ones. Like many of us, I don't like his liberalism or his transhumanism, but it's undeniable that the "hype" that fuels his investments is based on the significant chance that he can pull of what he says. Afterall everyone wrote off starlink and autonomous cars.

I do find it irritating how people can't talk about him or spacex in good faith. They hide behind investment language they don't understand when the truth is that they hate him and his companies because the media told them to. The regime media has had it in for him ever since he bought twitter since they had a near total monopoly on information before that point. You're seeing just how much they hate the situation by the fact that the UK grooming gang discussion in parliament was hijacked by deranged leftists into a 2 minute hate against Musk.
These same people never have a bad word to say about George Soros for example, a truly hideous "person" who has done nothing except harm humanity and steal money.

62362700
>>62361452
Now that he's a trilly, do you goys think this bitch will still settle for that $500k/year he was giving her or will that 10x soon?

62362803
>>62362689
>they hate him and his companies because the media told them to
I simply hate all billionaires because it's fucked

62362809
>>62362689
>The regime media
the ones who pushed EVs?
>These same people never have a bad word to say about George Soros for example
you mean the currency manipulator who "broke the bank of england" ?
who did he steal money from?

62362814
>>62361452
In a sea of grifting the guy offers at least a unique perspective on his multiple scams. For that he is awarded 1 trillion dollars

62362818
>>62361452
ask yourself which space sector company can dislodge SpaceX from government contract welfare. they are now too embedded, much like Microsoft with their shitty products, and will always recieve enough taxpayer gibs to keep them profitable while also paying investors. literally nobody else can compete

62362863
>>62361452
It's a mystery

62362883
>>62361478
He makes promises he can't keep and retards like you fall for it every time which keep the stocks inflated.

62362985
>>62361452
It's paper networth. His net worth is in stock shares. The stock market has nothing to do with real life and is just a popularity contest. Buying a share of Tesla and thinking you own a piece of Tesla despite it never paying a dividend or doing a buyback is no different than buying dodgecoin and thinking u own a stake in the dog. His stocks only value is just what ever u can tell to for to the next person. All he does is pull from science fiction and that keeps his stock popular. It's literally no different from a casino. Ultimately this gambling money comes from the petro dollar

item.title: How will this affect South Koreas economy? — 31 replies — 106.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
How will this affect South Koreas economy?

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-06-13 12:50 UTC

OP

How will this affect South Koreas economy?

Should I go all-in on a SK index fund?

Early replies (first 3)

62350030
>>62349989
I like that he recognizes bald suffering, but the issue is mostly rampant feminism, so effectively, he is an enabler.

62350349
>>62349989
by shaving you no longer have to worry about styling your hair and it's a repellent for nasty and bad women

62350516
>>62350349
>just bend over and let the norwood reaper fuck you in the ass

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62350557
in the usa you should be able to pay for finasteride and minoxidil with an HSA. hair is protection from skin cancer.

62350562
>>62350349
You dont look like a 90s skinhead, you skinnyfat onions baldy.

62350565
>>62350557
Almost everywhere in the world generic finasteride options cost like $20-30 for an entire years supply.

Are the pharma companies scamming burgers by overcharging finasteride like they do with most other meds?

62350592
Baldy, baldy over there...

62350594
>>62350565
Except fin is a tranny drug poison. Post finasteride syndrome reddit has 15k unique weekly visits.

62350624
>>62350594
About 20% of men between 18-35 (who arent on fin) develop ED.

When people who do take fin develop ED, of course the drug that nukes users dicks (temporarily) 1% of the time is what they'll blame it on, while in reality it's something else entirely. I'd bet the number of finasteride users who develop permanent ED is pretty close to 20%, the same percentage as men who don't use it.

62350707
>>62349989
Just make it socially acceptable to wear hats everywhere. I am balding and I just wear a hat all the time and I was still able to get laid. Basically by the time I make it to the point the hat comes off it is too late and the sex happens anyway. I'd imagine this is what it is like when trans women trick straight men into bed with them.

62350754
>>62350707

62350920
>>62349989
attractive men = baby boom

62351695
mandatory plastic surgery
everyone looks like kpop
big orgy ensues

62351707
>>62350594
Finasteride has a half life of 6 hours and PFS is a fake condition invented by American grifters and bald cucks.

62351757
>>62349989
good work mr president, that outta improve the birth rate

62352443
>>62350565
20 a year? I pay 9 a month
t. eufag

62352757
>>62352443
I’m eu too and I get a 98-pack of 5mg for about €35. Split the 5mg into 4 pieces and you have 392 doses.

Never heard of anyone actually being prescribed individual 1mg tablets here. Pretty much all doctors prescribe the 5mg option and tell them to split it in 4

62352787
>>62350565
no, it is also cheap in the US. it’s just annoying that you can’t buy it with an HSA. it’s also annoying that you need a prescription. should be OTC.

62355508
>>62350624
>>62351707
Literally everyone develops watery semen on it, have fun taking that poison that will fuck you up after some years, instead of just getting a toupe.

62355814
>>62355508
I've taken it for 4 years and never experienced that. Looked it up now and in clinical trials it was a side-effect that hit about 3% of users, and which went back to normal when they discontinued usage.

Tens of millions of men are on finasteride and like 95% of guys who go on the internet to talk about it are doing so because they had negative effects from it and need advice. It's like going to a subreddit about sexual assault and assuming that just because 90% of the people posting there got raped means that 90% of people in real life have been raped as well, when that is obviously far from true

62355979
>>62350349
*repellent for all women

62356219
The mistake most men make when jumping on finasteride is not getting their testosterone and DHT levels checked first. Since using a drug that nukes your DHT while having low T and by extension low DHT is a recipe for disaster.

Like 20% of men between 18-35 have clinically low T (under 300ng/dl) that warrants medical treatment. Up to 30% are in the danger zone (under 350ng/dl) with mild symptoms of low T. Any doctor who prescribes finasteride to these people and nuking their DHT levels even further deserve to lose their license for malpractice

62360477
>>62349989
>free hair transplants
>free lefort / bimax surgery
>free limb lengthening
>free boobjobs
>free rhinoplasty
>free miscellaneous cosmetic surgery
Honestly not a bad solution to a dying fertility rate

62360611
>>62350624
tranny well poisoner
no, dht is an important hormone and you cant just block it because your reddit sisters say the science is safe
>its not temporary
i have not taken it but i know dudes who have gotten permanently nooked form fin

62360635
>>62350754
I actually had this once, stopped me at the door and asked me to take my beanie off
I do so and he goes "DONT WORRY MATE!!! DONT WORRY, IM BALD TOO ITS OKAY"

62360696
>>62349989
shocked he's not focused on the teenie weenie epidemic.

62360743
>>62350707
Not watery semen at all. My shit works normal but I have zero opportunity to use it because fin isn't a miracle drug. I'm bald af. Still taking fin+min and using spray paint and hair fibers to fill gaps.

Maybe if I bust like 9 times in a short period of time might make it watery. My libido is crazy high. My favorite thing about fin is it prevents prostate issues. You might also find you are attracted to nearly every woman you encounter. So maybe expect heavier post nut clarity.

62360773
>>62350349
I don't have the right head shape to do that. I'd look fucked up even more than I already do.

62360967
>>62355814
did it make ur head smaller?

62362891
>>62355814
You will be on that crap for decades so enjoy the few good years left and you will see.

62362975
>>62360967
Clearly it shrunk his brain

item.title: how the FUCK can I invest in semiconductors and spacex from Moscow? — 18 replies — 68.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
how the FUCK can I invest in semiconductors and spacex from Moscow?

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-13 12:51 UTC

OP

how the FUCK can I invest in semiconductors and spacex from Moscow? This pozzed country doesnt let me invest in anything not even the russian stock market

Early replies (first 3)

62361955
move to thailand

62361960
>>62361945
>expecting wholesome gif
>it's someone dying
This is why you subhuman faggot what is wrong with you people?? When will you become civilised? Then you can join the rest of humanity at the investment table.

62361965
>>62361960
he was fine though just a little disoriented dont be a tranny

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62361977
>>62361945
Invest in it by overthrowing Putin for being a race traitor. Russia is importing 800,000 jeets annually. That's worse than the UK.

62361990
>>62361945
Don't you have domestic scams?

62361996
>>62361945
You can buy exposure to most of the big semi stocks + spcx on hyperliquid. Non-KYC (for now) so just get a vpn and some usdc and go for it, if that's what you want to do. It's a perps exchange so you don't really own the stock, on the other hand it's a perps exchange so you can leverage your 50 rubles into 250 rubles of exposure if you want to.

62362003
>>62361996
thanks for the tip

62362010
>>62361945
In Soviet Russia, company owns you!

62362035
>>62361945
an hero and hope that you come back as an American in the next life

62362336
>>62361945
move to Zelenograd

62362422
Cock blocking nanny state

62362531
Can russians not even use ibkr?

62362698
>>62361960
Russians and Eastern Europeans are living the most depressed lives
Dying is considered a reprieve in those countries

62362738
>>62361945
Tell Putin he sucks ass, escape Puccia, do whatever you want with your life.

Or go die in a trench in Ukraine, your choice.

62362752
Its another indian meme thread, There is no reason why he wouldn't be able to invest in the Russian stock market, or in local gold and bond products (unless he is actually a muslim migrant with no citizenship). Moreover there are international stock brokers that do accept Russians. Finally for Russian it might be a better idea to invest in Cambricon and SMIC, than to put money in jewish stock markets.

62362754
>>62361945
I don't know maybe start caring and discussing about politics instead treating it as something you have absolutely no influence on like a fucking weather.

62362757
>>62362698
im bulgarian and i live better than you

62362976
>>62362757
>lel the table-let thinks he's living better than me
I have a full-sized table and one of those rugs too.

item.title: Law of the jungle — 65 replies — 45.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Law of the jungle

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-06-13 11:41 UTC

OP

>I am a million billion trillionair-
>BAM BAM BAM
>STAB STAB STAB
W-WHY AM I DYING I AM RICH AND HAVE MONEY ROCKETS CARS AIEEEEEE
P-PLEASE SIRE SAVE M-
>BAM BAM BAM
NOOOO WHY IS MONEY USELESS WHY WHY WHY WAAAAH PLEASE SIR
I WILL GIVE YOU EVERYTHIN-
>STAB HIS KIDS
AAAAAAH MY KIDDIES MY BLOODLINE WHY WHY
PLEASE SIR THE TRILLION MONEY
ACK

Early replies (first 3)

62359354
>>62359336
a mentally buck busted normie is going to say """the law""" grants the rich physical immunity.... and even if it doesn't, they have like, body guards who are omnipotent, omniscient, and omnipresent. you can't do ANYTHING to the rich!!!! it's literally impossible!!!!!

62359360
>>62359336
We live in capitalism and I'm protected by the pigs u need to learn how to move nigga

62359387
>nigger tier envy thread
>/biz/

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62361033
>>62360991
The most important social contract is the Ten Commandments.
It's the commies and anarchist left that does away with them.

62361047
>>62360991
> rich defenders
Wtf is that even? Since when do intelligence and/or success deserve to be seen as punishable negatives? What's there to defend about being rich? Are you insane?

62361048
>>62360896
generational wealth? you make $200k and a $2 million net worth. lmao.

62361065
>>62360991
literally just pay him to chill. its that easy

62361074
>>62359496
Those are airsoft shit.

62361079
>>62360200
>kekaroo
This gay aussie /pol/ faggot spams /biz/
Lovely.

62361121
get a trillion dollars and find out what happens
oh, nothing happens? ok

62361214
I know a god will hunting troll when I see one.

62361399
>>62359496
These are BB guns, brownoid.
Google what that is, since you don't know.

62361400
>>62359336
Based

62361423
>>62361048
2 mil in stocks/crypto not 2 mil net worth and unlike you I don't need to cry and piss my pants over rich people

62361436
kinda rude

62361646
>>62361423
plywood box equity doesn't count. and you aren't rich, because you work for a living. literally no one in the history of mankind has created generational worth from working. you need to own a business, which you don't.

62361649
>>62361646
if he has that much in stocks and crypto he probably owns a business.

all you have to do is buy and sell in a single fiscal year and you're technically in business.

62361653
>>62359336
Nigger, you will never do shit, you never have done shit. Your impotent crying school shooter fantasy will not improve you situation, while your mother is getting rutted by the tenth nigger of the day

62361673
>>62360694
kek poorfag and a stinkoid brownoid baggie. i am so much better than you its unreal

62361674
>>62361653
>nigger cuck bbc fantasy posting while defending trillionaire jews online
i am whiter than you

62361730
>>62360200
>>62361079
Interesting fact about "Kekaroo":

Not once in the history of the word "Kekaroo" did the person typing it actually laughed. It's the ultimate "I'm not mad, I'm laughing" internet argument seethecope word.

62361735
>>62361674
>i am whiter than you
>things white people don't say
there's no "whiter"
you either are white or you are not.

you are not

62361872
>>62359336
if you were rich you wouldn't live around the jungle.

62361876
>how do you respond without sounding mad?
BANG BANG BANG BANG

The answer is a concealed carry.

62361887
>>62359400
It's a sneaking mission, then.

62362019
>>62359400
Right. They live in secluded, secure compounds, travel privately, etc. etc.

62362087
>>62361730
>he types this with his brown hands with tears in his eyes
kekaroo

62362109
>>62362087
You're not even cracking a smile right now

62362126
>>62359336
I wish stupid people would just stick to being beasts of burden and let the smart people do the things that make society go. He doesnt have a fucking scrooge mcduck money bank brainlet, its company valuation. paper value.

62362156
>>62359336
because billionaires cant buy armies...

62362331
>>62359336
If you ever matter enough for a truly wealthy elite to want you dead, they could have it within the day. Lucky for you, impotent rage fantasy posting on biz indicates you're a nobody.

62362865
>>62359336
>I WILL GIVE YOU EVERYTHIN-
will you suck my dick?
will you eat my shit?

62362874
>>62361735
There are degrees of Whiteness you fucking retard. My couple of girl cousins have a puerto rican dad and they're fair skinned with light bnwo hair and bnwo eyes

item.title: >OMG MUSK IS SO RICH FIRST TRILLIONAIRE o algo meanwhile geRICHmany — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>OMG MUSK IS SO RICH FIRST TRILLIONAIRE o algo meanwhile geRICHmany

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-13 13:04 UTC

OP

>OMG MUSK IS SO RICH FIRST TRILLIONAIRE o algo
meanwhile geRICHmany

Early replies (first 3)

62362887
best way to win with inflation is debt :)

if the dollar hyperinflates, imagine your car/house payment being relative fucking pennies. that's what happened in argentina, maybe the niggerdebtmaxxers were right.

62362947
>>62362834
Bread's price has increased only by 30% in the last 10 years.
Elon's wealth increased from $11b to $1.1 trillion, a 10,000% increase.

62362953
>>62362834
Did they have to use a modern image of African shitskins pushing the literal wheelbarrow of money though? I thought there were pics like this under Weimar

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62362955
>>62362953
Bro thinks pictures that are on black in white are modern

62362980
>>62362955
That photo has black people and a sign in english. It's clearly not 1920s Germany.

62362984
>>62362887
This is the US Government's strategy unironically.
It's why Trump immediately fired Powell and went to elect a puppet who will not raise rates for any time he's in office: He wants to strangle the USD so the massive debt being incurred right now becomes irrelevant.

62362998
>>62362984
it's either that or print a 45 trillon coin + tip to expunge the debt, both are bad the dollar

62363002
>>62362953
>>62362980

Zimbabwe most likely.
>>62362834
That's cute, trillion dollars ain't what it used to be. But you'll find he has greater purchasing power than really anyone in the modern era, with the rest being kinda fictional or hard to express

item.title: The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants. — 43 replies — 31.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants.

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-06-13 11:45 UTC

OP

The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants. He's upper class. And has 3 passports and owns properties and businesses (wealth) around the world. He isn't waiting on a paycheck to drive down to Costco.
Hint: it's a Rothschild property.
Making even a million bucks a year writing code for Elon or Bezos or Brin does NOT put you in the upper class. At all.
I am European and only we have class. We invented class.

Early replies (first 3)

62356062
>>62356048
Before AI, you could write code for ten years and then buy this.

62356086
Got so mad in the other thread you thought you would start a new thread about it LMAO

62356191
this is that same polish guy who was seething in the other thread about class or whatever

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62358606
>>62356048
>AAAACKKKKKKK

62358695
>>62356048
That place looks like a demonic kike festival. Hard pass. Heres a life tip. Don't get caught up with material things or worldly possessions. It looks nice but the people who live such extravagant lives are always empty morally corrupt husks and their children are absolute degenerates. Instead of aspiring to live like rich jews. Aspire spiritual wealth. Talk to God. Meditate on the words of Jesus Christ. Walk on the narrow road that leads to life and do not be mislead by the idolatrous jewish elites that reject the Living God and the Truth

62358945
>>62358433
I need to pay the bills
>>62358695
Based. You are not far from the Kingdom of God, but don't kid yourself, there's still a lot to do

62358989
>>62358606
>"Death is the great equalize...-ACCKKK!!!"

62359005
>>62358989

62359167
>>62356048
>dickriding gloating by association
>this lifestyle was real in my mind
Cool story, op
Lunch is over, time to clock back in

62359177
>>62358695
Based

62359194
>>62358695
>Jesus Christ
>demonic kike

62359210
>>62358989
It still is, but you can live your life like a beta peasant trying to extend your life seeking a philosopher stone (if only to perpetuate your suffering) or live like a chad pharaoh accepting death and building his own sarcophagus and tomb during his lifetime. Two different games, yet the same ending

62359212
>>62358695
Where the fuck is Christ? Why has he foresaken me? Is he even fucking real?

62359254
>>62358989
You don't even know the name of the guy who built those.

62359275
>>62356048
yeah and he is never happy and is made of the same carbon we all are. class is fake. wealth is fake. you can be happy with nothing easier than being happy with everything.

62359283
>>62359212
the real god made himself known to man a lot longer than 2000 years ago. it was pre history, so of course we know nothing about it. abrahamic tradition is nonsense.

62359313
>>62356048
Damn I triggered this barely literate 2nd generation immigrant to America (note I never called him an American - he isn't) so bad. Damn, I am almost sorry since it's Friday but not sorry since you're brown.

62359371
>>62356048
I'm sure his life sucks everyone has a reason to cry about shit no amount of stucco will save your body from pain suffering and misery it is inescapable

62359452
>>62358695
God bless you please pray for me.

62359520
>class is following as many rules as possible while frowning and spending lots of money
Wow sounds like a good time sign me up

62359522
>>62358695
Oh please spare me the weak cope philosophy where Jesus christ literally gets abandoned by God when he needs him the most on the cross so there is no guarantee. God is a child's fantasy of cohesion that the world is simple and suffering has meaning, you think someone is watching you at all times and ur not smart enough to realize that emptiness is structural and is born out of language and it's a insurmountable gap that you can only move through and not ever reach. When u move towards it without expecting anything..that is when u can finally act finally be free from god at least,.to relate to the symbolic world of language differently in a way where u can actually do what you want.

62359740
>>62359522
You’re a fag or a drug or porn addict. Maybe all three. Fuck off retard.

62360058
>>62359212
>>62359283
"The fool says in his heart that there is no God"

Maybe try turning away your sinful ways, repenting and searching scripture instead of looking for handouts. Or you could just live out your days as a hard hearted rancid transgressor and go to the grave an unbelieving fool. Nothing anyone says can change your blackened heart. The Holy Spirit does that work.

62360102
>>62360058
why is a 24 year old mulatto born again christian zoomer trying to give me life advice?

62360116
>>62359522
you have free will to do what you want. But be warned that you lash out at the unblemished Lamb of God. The Alpha and the Omega who healed every infirmity and cast out demons from the land. The Good Shepherd who has defeated and and is Seated at the right hand of Power. The sad thing about people like you is that you are so fallen and your nature is so corrupt that you cannot even hear the Truth if it was read to you. Even the demons themselves that Jesus drove out of people KNEW that he was the Son of the Most High. Repent brother. The Kingdom of Heaven is at hand. We know not the day nor the hour of His return

62360814
>>62359005
>these death monuments will be renouned and respected and not some dinky little tourist hotsp-ACK

62361163
>>62357608
Why do people hate on these posts instead of try to figure out ways to improve their quality of life to have similar?

That's the whole point of this board. Instead of hating on people showing off their wealth figure out how to get it too.

I thought it was only like this on hip hop forums. Everyone hat s on each other and then refuse all ways of getting to it.

62361183
>>62359210
Or stop all or nothing thinking and find a way to live well so you can have wealth and not have to compromise yourself in any way?

62362527
>>62356048
Why would I want such a monstrosity.

62362541
>>62360116
>unfathomably based life speaking minister of /biz
I will miss you when the tranny kike mods ban you for speaking truth, but I know you will bear your cross with dignity.

62362654
>>62356048
why would you even want this? what are you going to use it for? throw parties 24/7 for people you fucking hate? If you live their 24/7 the nice view and garden just become mundane like everything else only now you have a lot less money

62362675
>>62362527
You’re not European, you would not get it.

62362885
>>62358695
>stay poor and let the Jews control wealth and power like a good goy

item.title: What's wrong with just DCA'ing BTC, ETH, and SOL and not worrying about what its doing? — 4 replies — 11.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
What's wrong with just DCA'ing BTC, ETH, and SOL and not worrying about what its doing?

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-13 12:46 UTC

OP

What's wrong with just DCA'ing BTC, ETH, and SOL and not worrying about what its doing?

Early replies (first 3)

62362176
Nothing wrong with it, but you should be using the ETH or SOL to bet on a memecoin thats <100k mc that you think has a chance of going to 100M-1B mc

62362880
>>62362160
eth, sol, ada, link, all the shitcoins are going to zero.
you should only hold btc, perhaps some monero if you feel frisky.

62362941
>>62362160
This is essentially what I did between 2018 and 2024 (bought BTC, ETH, and little bit of LINK/AAVE/ADA). I made it and no longer go to work.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62362966
>>62362160
Why didn't the alligator fight back?

item.title: SPCX is the new bitcoin. — 8 replies — 10.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
SPCX is the new bitcoin.

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-13 12:20 UTC

OP

SPCX is the new bitcoin.

Early replies (first 3)

62362526
>>62362506
You honestly might be right

62362529
>>62362506
So it's finna crash hard?

62362543
Nah nah nah, the tam is saying each share is worth $22,000. We are in chipotle hours now!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62362551
I'll give you my DD.

I was watching Bloomberg this entire week. Every single day they avoided talking about SPCX. Everytime it was brought up, they said it was worth nothing.

Even early in the morning the day of the IPO, they kind of didn't talk about it until open. They didn't want to show Elon playing rocket man and so on. I kept watching Bloomberg and then something weird started happening. The hosts started getting really angry and salty. They kept bring up blue origin as a threat if they ever IPO'd and wanted to not talk about spacex anymore.

I continued to watch and eventually interviews started coming in where boombers were angry that they didn't get shares allocated to them and this Business Professor at the duke university got red in the face and said he was going to tell his university MBA students about this sham of an IPO. He rose up 1 finger and said "that's all I got, 1 share!"

That's when I knew this was the new bitcoin. It is born out of butthurt.

62362914
Mark my words. SPCX is going to $80k.

62362917
They hold bitcoin on the balance sheet

62362933
>>62362506
yep, -70% is programmed

62362934
>>62362506
No it's not

item.title: I want to buy SpaceX but it's midday Saturday in the UK and the market is closed. — 6 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I want to buy SpaceX but it's midday Saturday in the UK and the market is closed.

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-13 12:22 UTC

OP

I want to buy SpaceX but it's midday Saturday in the UK and the market is closed.

Early replies (first 3)

62362877
If you want to replicate the experience just take whatever you were going to invest, and put it directly in the burn barrel

62362888
>>62362877
But other anons here bought thousands and are celebrating.

62362890
>>62362868
that's probably considered a hate crime in bongistan

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62362894
>>62362868
you can buy perpetual contracts on binance.

62362900
>>62362888
Just wait a week or two, and you’ll be the one celebrating for not being the exit liquidity for Musky boy and his friends during SpaceX’s multigenerational stock price collapse.

62362938
>>62362900
actually, no. those people will get to dump too.

the actual bagholders will be every single person with a retirement account once the fasttrack goes through

item.title: You MUST post in this thread if you are waging rn — 78 replies — 28.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
You MUST post in this thread if you are waging rn

Open thread • Replies: 78 • Updated: 2026-06-13 10:24 UTC

OP

wage

Early replies (first 3)

62351330
I'm currently hiding in the bathroom

62351353
>>62351325
Im not desu

62351358
>>62351325
i hate being a wagie so fucking much

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62354681
I'm sitting on the toilet now while waging.

I always take multiple shits per day while being paid for it.

62354699
>>62354675
It’s such a joke. Trucking is a nightmare, no wonder jeets do it

62354715
>>62352824
I can't or I get in biiiiiiig trouble

62354716
>>62351325
I being paid to sleep in a hotel because tradies keep fucking up the infrastructure for our machine

62354761
>>62354699
Can't believe how many jets have jumped into the industry. I feel like the average normie isn't aware of their presence in that sector.

62354806
>>62354761
People have no idea how much danger they are in on the highway because of the shitjeets. It’s an existential threats to everybody

62354992
>>62354661
Idk maybe I'm weak but I need to "decompress" after work

62355007
>>62354992
> Idk maybe I'm weak but I need to "decompress" after work
Same. It is so draining.

62356088
I have alot of down time at my workplace too
big issue for me is a 2 hour commute every day but I put up with it since my rent is cheap

62356156
>>62353364
Ash Grove. In that pic I was troubleshooting a truck loading issue. I discovered that our loading system was haunting loading due to some shutdown condition for a split second and tgen deciding to be happy again, most likely fluctuations in the scale wait from the truck or scale shaking while loading. The momentary blip breaks a logic latch forcing a retard of the loading process each time. Now I get to find out how to use timer off delays in unity pro and proworx32 to filter out that momentary blip. Every day is a new problem I have to figure out and I enjoy that.

62356177
>>62356156
I like how my phone knows that I call people retards on 4chan so often it autocorrect a typo of reload to retard

62356574
I'm shitting

62356712
I have an HR adult daycare job. I’m busy for maybe 40 hours a month. Most of my free time is spent shitposting here or reading the news. Today the only thing I have going on is a 1 hour meeting in the afternoon.
>>62356088
I just got my MBA, mostly paid for by the job. I used pretty much all the downtime I typically had to do coursework at work. Shit was so cash.

62356735
I'm taking a break and sitting on a bench at the Bass Pro Shop museum in Dania Beach

62356749
I'm wfh, haven't done anything all afternoon except shitpost on /biz/ and do some laundry

62356784
I'm trying to hire a line cook but nobody wants to work. Need to find a make work adult daycare job.

62357448
>>62356712
How do you get a bullshit HR job if you are a dude? Are you brown?

62357470
>>62351325
I hope we all make it and never have to do this shit again

62357873
I'm at the airport lounge enjoying booze and snacks. Today is remote work day, just finished a teams call, now I just need to wrap up the week by spending about a minute to update one confluence item.

62358052
>>62351325
Nah I live in Europe so I'm done for the week. 100% WFH cybersecurity. Shit's so cash.

62358146
>>62357448
I’m White.
I got lucky and was hired by the HR department’s budget manager, who is also a white male.
My role is more that of an accountant than what you would think of as an HR role, but in the company registry I’m technically HR. My boss (that same budget manager) and I handle the HR department’s finances and try to keep the roasties on budget. They’re pretty deferential, and my boss is very hands on, so that doesn’t leave me with a lot to do beyond monthly reconciliation reports and paying some bills as they come up.
It’s a sweet gig, the only shit part is that it pays peanuts.

62358716
>>62356712
>Today the only thing I have going on is a 1 hour meeting in the afternoon
And they just canceled it, lel. My weekend is starting early.

62359402
>>62351325
I'm in class, but I'm not paying attention to it.

62359670
>>62351325
wrong image OP

WAGE

62360176
>>62351868
I want to visit Thailand.

62360948
>>62356735
Holy comfy

62360968
>>62354378
How?

62361451
I legitimately don't think about my job at work 80% of the time as I'm usually busy browsing /biz/, playing online Catan or talking to Claude while "working". The other 20% is me trying to figure out how I asking out my coworker so obviously likes me, but without the chance of having an awkward conversation with HR before my termination.

62362712
I worked on a quick Mars wargame scenario to de-rad today. It was nil casualties and the pursuit craft went kamikaze on a...pfft...thorium mine mistaking it for Hutchie HQ...idiot.

62362766
>>62354589
third wordie here, I'm teaching kids english online, shitpay but at the least I get to slack off behind the screen if the kid is doing ok. Had been trying to find better deals but my brain is currently fried from gaming so doing this to stall out my situation in the meantime.

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 93 replies — 23.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 93 • Updated: 2026-06-13 09:07 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62352277
>>62351090
Hmmm, some people are here for the product, some are here for the chart. They're not always the same crowd.

62352286
>>62350315
Ethereum has certainly built one of the strongest ecosystems in crypto.

62354098
>>62351129
You have a point there

62354803
>>62350283
Maybe this is how people finally start exploring BTC in DeFi. Participation is still tiny. Imagine what happens if even 1% starts becoming 2%, then 5%.

62354820
>>62351106
I've actually earned more from memecoins than utility coins lately lol.

62354827
>>62339328
Anyone bullish on avalanche?

62355050
>>62339328
I sold when the Epstein files dropped. Feels good.

62355133
>>62354803
That’s the part a lot of people overlook. If TBVs actually work as intended, I think people are underestimating how much TVL native BTC use cases could pull in.

62355136
>>62355050
Time will tell whether it was the right call, but at least you acted according to your move.

62355617
>>62355133
It's crazy that DeFi security is still one of the biggest problems in the space.

62355631
>>62354827
Buy avax today and sell @ $100

62355632
>>62339417
Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
And monero is based on bitcoin. The entire crypto market exists because of all the money in bitcoin which is now backed by the US government.

62356269
>>62355617
At this point, thorough audits and multiple layers of review should be the standard, not a nice bonus anon.

62356538
>>62355631
What's your basis?

62357046
>>62345600
Probably the better route. We've seen too many centralized services blow up over the years, so you can't really blame people for preferring fully decentralized setups.

62357057
>>62355632
What about ZEC?

62357077
ICP

62357115
>>62339328
I sold at 116k I dont regret it at all and have about 2 mil waiting for sept-dec buy in.

62357126
>>62339328
I can never regret buying BTC desu, I bought back in around 70K and will DCA more if price goes below 60K. I'm also staking a little BTC on babylon for some extra yield

62357923
>>62355632
>Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
yeah just like the bitcoin developers right? they're the ones who control bitcoin?

it's a good question though, what if they were? miners would have to agree on consensus on which code to accept. if github ever refused to host monero's code then it would just move to .onion or .i2p

62359525
>>62357046
That's how it goes. They've already got GoMining, Ledger, and a16z involved, so let's see who they bring in next.

62359541
>>62357057
ZEC is interesting. If the privacy narrative picks up again, it's usually one of the first names people bring up.

62359557
>>62339328
I literally don't care about it. Mogged by virtually everything else over the last couple of years.

Also why is faggot shit like dogecoin still worth billions of dollars.

>in b4 bottom signal
Probably.

62359588
>>62357126
Sounds like you've got a plan and you're sticking to it. Hard to ask for much more than that. I wouldn't be surprised if more people follow suit over time. The $4B+ TVL shows there's already plenty of interest.

62359608
>>62359557
The market has a weird habit of rewarding the things nobody can rationally explain.

62359673
>>62357077
People still remember the launch, but the tech discussion around ICP is often more interesting than the price chart.

62360479
>>62359557
When bottom

62361782
>>62360479
Nobody knows

62362657
>>62359557
How do you mean?

62362661
>>62339328
While BTC is in deep bear stage, it's time to get in. I need to be greedy when others are fear.

item.title: >DUDE JUST BUY A HOUSE LMAO what if your corrupt local government approves several AI data centers and they get built 10… — 37 replies — 21.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
>DUDE JUST BUY A HOUSE LMAO what if your corrupt local government approves several AI data centers and they get built 10…

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-06-13 11:24 UTC

OP

>DUDE JUST BUY A HOUSE LMAO
what if your corrupt local government approves several AI data centers and they get built 100 feet away from your house? why would i want to risk this?
>THAT WILL NEVER HAPPEN
it's happening all over the US.

Early replies (first 3)

62358792
>don't buy a house!!
>what if someone builds a wearhouse and puts gpus inside of it!!!
really retard?

62358798
>>62358792
the "what if" could be extended to any number of scenarios.
>halfway house gets built next door
>neighbor turns his SFH into a section ape rental

62358803
>>62358792
>>62358798
also the word is "warehouse" you 60 IQ simian niggerbeast.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62359055
>>62358781
Texas is so Cucked. Water and the energy grid are already wrecked here

62359072
>>62359055
free market forces will fix it, ese.

62359311
I wish data centers got built but nimbys too strong

That tax rev too good
>but muh water bill
Oh no! My 40$ water bill will be 60$

62359330
New england chads rise up

62359374
>>62358792
they are loud and take resources from everyone around them. the power grid and water supply are not infinite

62359386
>>62359311
these data centers are getting multi year tax abatements. and when those abatements run out, you need to be literally retarded to think that local government will lower individual income taxes to offset the pickup in business taxes. it has literally never, EVER happened.

62359398
>>62358803
>he doesn't know about Gun's Wearhouse
ngmi

62359460
>>62359386
I live in the free world so my income tax is already 0

62359499
>>62359460
here you >>62359311 gleefully dream about increased tax revenues while apparently paying nothing, leading me to believe you're a poverty stricken white nigger on gibs.

62359743
>>62358781
importing a million indians at each blue dot

62359751
Get fucked! Ai is the future

62359755
>>62358781
>oh no, major corporate industrial investment in my local area that could create a lot of jobs, this is terrible!
t. retired boomers who don't care if shitty retail jobs and meth labs are the only sources of employment for the younger generations

62359791
>>62359499
Kys chang you will never be leader in ai

62359886
>>62359755
i lived in northeastern PA during the marcellus shale "boom." most of the jobs went to out of state workers and, 15 years later, all that's left of the "boom" in my hometown is poisoned wells and a few 3 story hotels that are owned by indians. if you still believe in the industrial job creation meme in the year 1980+46 you are literally retarded.

62360158
>>62359886
My PA mountain house land gets me 100k a year through fracking and I only go there to hunt a couple times a year. Thanks for playing sweety

62360489
>>62360158
limp wristed LARP tell. fracking is a process, not a metric on which royalties are paid. i know this because my parents are paid monthly royalties on a well that sits on their property. they also decrease hyperbolically (the well is re-worked) and so most people (including my parents) are getting basically nothing these days. last time they mentioned it to me, it was something like $100 a month.

62360795
>>62358781
>Those massive circles in Utah
>The cluster in Arizona
Where's all the water going to come from?

62360996
>>62360489
>they mentioned it to me, it was something like $100 a month.
You're parents sound like retards kek. Washington County stays winning. Until I take all it's resources lmao

62361000
>>62360795
Uh buddy I don't know how to tell you this but water comes from the wall. Or a hose if your wall has a hose.

62361003
>>62360795
>Where's all the water going to come from?

how much water do server and electronics need

62361006
>>62358781
IPO was a success. nothing you can do but seethe, lol.

62361042
>>62358781
surprised they wouldn't use the loopholes to take your house too. you don't really own anything if you aren't close to the people in power

62361046
>>62359755
Datacenters create incredibly few jobs actually. They increase work during construction, then hire an H1B skeleton crew.

62362112
>>62360795
They need to ban evaporation cooling for data centers. Let them do the extra steps to either radiate the heat into the air or run their hot water back into the grid.
>>62361003
Most of these data centers use evaporation towers to get rid of the massive amounts of heat those GPUs generate. This is the cheapest way to cool those data centers, especially when corrupt local government provides you with cheap fresh water.

62362134
Why doesn’t the orange retard just take Greenland and put the data centers in the ice

62362146
>>62358781
>tfw used to live in NoVa 7 years ago
I used to live 5 miles away from a chilled beverage distribution center and the monotonous everpresent EEEEEEEEEEEEEEEENNNNHHHH their chillers made pissed me off to no end for 3 years straight, day and night, no holidays off. If I had to deal with 10 of those at once within the vicinity of my home things would get very very violent very very quickly.

62362171
>>62358781
How long until data center construction constitutes eminent domain?

62362554
>>62358781
I’m surprised the west coast has such a small amount of centers. Hippies aren’t so bad after all
Texas looks fucked. Thank god they don’t have all those rules and regulations of California
Kek

62362585
Idaho it is

62362850
>>62362112
data centers use globally in one year the same amount of water the US uses for agriculture in a single day
>they need to ablooablooo
they need to stop listening to retards
>>62362554
california is one the most water strained states in the US and they waste it on planting almonds for said hippies.

62362747
looks like shit

sure if you can scam some subhumans but you're competing with 1 billion jeet scammers

An
Ad
!

62362753
>>62362737
reminds me of the good old days.

62362775

62362806
>>62362737

62362843
The overall background changes the further I walk away.
Now it's broken.

62362844

62362866
>>62362843

kek

62362946

item.title: SWIFT confirms Chainlink as the bedrock of their systems — 92 replies — 13.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
SWIFT confirms Chainlink as the bedrock of their systems

Open thread • Replies: 92 • Updated: 2026-06-13 06:30 UTC

OP

Kek fudniggers

Early replies (first 3)

62354945
>Inb4 source
https://www.icmagroup.org/assets/documents/Regulatory/Repo/ICMA-report-DLT-and-repo-part-I-June-2026-040626.pdf

62354971
it's all so tiring
i have sold much more of my stack than i wanted assuming that price would react by now

62354976
>>62354943
Cool, when will I be rich?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62359913
>>62355034
>When clarity act allows staking pools to expand,

bahahahhaha

bro what current regulation out there right now says you can have a staking pool but it can't grow?

what specific provision in the proposed clarity act is it that you think saves your special altcoin?


how are people still falling for this

62359988
>>62359501
>I have gone through so much turmoil

look at this kid talking about how hard it was to sit at a computer and read buzz words
your grandfather would be disgusted

> and learning while holding this damned token

bro you haven't learned shit, except that a fool and his money are soon parted

62360040
>>62359988
yes link marines like to pretend they are on the samurai warrior's mystical journey of self-discovery or something lmao

62360066
>>62354976
When the "antichrist" system comes into effect, I guess.
Assuming you survive whatever they have planned between now and then.

62360120
>>62360040
yeah it's pathetic


i would pay to see some of these boyscouts give a live presentation in their own words about one of those infographics like the one in op.
the chainlink boyscout revels in the fact that their cult investment comes off as so high iq that no one can understand it, including themselves if they're being honest.

the position for them is essentially a vanity hold, where they can fantasize about the day they show the world their misunderstood, off the charts intelligence. the problem is of course, that day isn't real

their hubris has been quite costly over the years

62360137
>>62359988
>>62360040
Ill pray for you two (probably one person on multiple ID’s)

62360230
>>62360137
we don't need your prayers stinkbag
we're doing just fine in the material realm without the assistance of your supposed "son of God" who was born in "immaculate conception" (lmao cope for Mary being a whore)

62360247
>>62355053
Three more years.... then my real life can begin

62360331
>>62360230
Im praying still

62360405
>>62360331
well stop

62360577
>>62355053
Yjk this nigger typed 2 more years but decided to throw an extra year in there because it would be too on the nose. Evil fucking nigger kek

62360584
>>62355583
>Flat feet bug gf poster is back
>Link down another -30% since
Sorry bro you better get used to the flapjack feets

62360663
>>62354976
did you take the vax?

62360802
>>62360066
What if link is actually worth something but you can't use it to buy bread. Like kinda like if you had an entire continent in your name but you still need the knowledge to extract resources, work with local governing bodies etc etc

Basically what if we have tokens but are unable to use them because we're goyim

62360825
>>62360137
>duuuuuude it's all a consssssssthhhhpirathy!!!

there he is everyone, our very own sleuth

excuse me, DETECTIVE sleuth

nothing, and i mean NOTHING, gets by this guy. he can sniff out colluding posts in seconds. this guy is GOOD, and i feel ashamed at ever trying to get one by him

afterall, everyone knows that there's nothing more important than guarding the reputation of a dead altcoin on a dead forum


well, you know what we say here...

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62360858
>>62360802
Well, then we're fucked either way.

62360859
>>62360825
>>62360405
Jesus loves you

62360865
>>62360859
religion is the opiate of the masses
or in this case, the stink marines

62360873
>>62360865
That doesn’t change that fact that I don’t hate you and I forgive you.

62360876
>>62360873
your "forgiveness" is rejected, stinkie
how does that make you feel

62361417
>>62360876
feels to me like you're gonna take my whole cock in your ass, and you're gonna like it.

62361431
>>62354976
2030

This year was always the plan for chainlink

62361469
>>62360876
Lilla My, why do you have so much hatred for stinkies?

62361506
I thought green man sergay was going to make me rich...

62361627
>>62358895
COPE

62361699
>>62354943
7 years
7 dollars

62361781
>>62361773

62362456
it's very easy, either it works or I'll keep my stack as a lesson in humility

there is no scenario where I sell

62362461
>>62362456
cool beta attitude, stinker
just admit you lost the game of el lifio

item.title: So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. — 63 replies — 7.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo.

Open thread • Replies: 63 • Updated: 2026-06-13 04:14 UTC

OP

So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. How the fuck is it legal for them to destroy so much shareholder value? I WANT MY FUCKING MONEY BACK NOW!!

Early replies (first 3)

62348099
>>62348037
You did not lose anything, shoe shine boy. change your diapers and calm the FUCK down. Because you didn't sell yet. Right?

62348149
Better price to buy m8. You don't believe in the business anymore or somethin

62348170
If you couldn't tell the vidya industry is dead, I don't know what to tell you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62354555
>>62354514
Millennial. I haven't been keeping up with what is going on the video game industry lately. Because I hardly play anymore. The thing is researching machine parts companies is tedious. Nintendo and Ubisoft are practically rock bottom low and easier to understand what they do. Already made money off of mining stocks. Just a smart poor fag being a poor fag carefully picking out what to invest in. Because I can't invest in everything I want to on my wishlist.

62354579
>>62352012
They ran out of feasible gimmicks that's why it's been the same shit repackaged since wiiU

62354587
Hahaha, are you the fucking retard that shilled them before the direct?
Classic!

62354609
I'm buying at this level, thanks for helping me fill my position bro

62357401
>>62354552
people have lost their creativity. nothing new like these classics come out, only slop using the brand of something people recognize but no one out there creating new zeldas and pokemons

62357443
>>62348037
Nintendo is sitting on an assload of cash. they don't care about shareholders.

62360010
>>62348170
Nintendo is going to continue doing well, but it was overpriced before.
Videogames are not a dying industry, they are just no longer a growth industry. The reason gaming used to seem like an amazing investment oportunity until recently was that there were way more people going into the hobby than going out and the first big generations of gamers were hitting their mid/late 30s when earnings start peaking. It didn't matter whether game companies were good or not because the gaming pie was growing so fast that their slice would grow too.
Now that gaming is as normiefied as it gets and there are adult gamers of all ages, the pie is not growing anymore and companies have to actually be good businesses if they want to survive and grow. That's why companies that were already known for a decade to be shit like Ubisoft seem to be imploding all of a sudden.

62360025
>>62349964
Now look at the 5 year returns for Advantest and Tokyo Electron and say that again.

62360035
>>62350352
>>all good games are made by indie developers
>why would you bet on any of the big companies? they are creatively bankrupt bloated bureaucracies
Even if most good games are made by smaller developers nowadays, most actual money printing game franchises that actually move the needle when it comes to investing are still big corporate IPs.

62360245
>>62360010
This is one of the reasons why I am hesitant to invest in gaming. Feels like I would be ripping off young first time investors who pump those stocks when a popular game comes out. Like OP. Anything industrial it is often boomers who died of old age bag holding before their relatives could retrieve their stock portfolios at the right time. Or they do retrieve a whale's when it is crabbing and there is a dip to be had. I get less guilt on my conscious screwing over a Boomer than a Zoomer.

62360284
>>62360245
Zoomers are getting cocky and obnoxious, acting like concepts they come up with haven't ever been heard of and tried before. I await the day for the alphas to grow up and shit all over them.

62360459
>>62360284
Still better than Boomers and gen X ever were. And that is saying something. I have more in common with Zoomer 20 years younger than me than a lead maxed gen X who is 10 years older. Very refreshing to have lots more people to talk to without the lead brain damage. Haven't heard as many good ideas circulating widely until they grew up.

Seriously Gen X were scum who often didn't even give a shit their own kids needed to be potty trained until a kindergarten teacher told them they needed to be. Most of whom were totally normal kids of average intelligence they neglected that badly when it came to teaching them everyday things. This is who were most of the Zoomers parents. They could have turned out better if it weren't for that. Every other generation was horrified by 4 year olds still in diapers. They really didn't care like a drugged out hippy until the kid was old enough to go to public school.

62360515
>>62348037
No one guys Nintenslop anymore. Millennials are the last generation that cares about it, but are ageing out of gaming now.

62360525
You shouldnt buy a stock unless you plan on keeping it for 5 to 10 years

62360531
>>62360459
That's cool and all, but those fags will die off soon, and then they'll just end up shifting blame to kikes or anyone else besides themselves. Not to mention this faggy generation is just as tech illiterate and censorious as the boomers they bitch about.

62360534
0 mainline heavy hitters yet. Metroid was in dev hell for a decade.

62360549
>>62348278
>They are getting cooked by real handhelds now and I’m going to be buying another handheld isn’t a switch for my son.
Such as? The steam deck is now overpriced and other handheld PCs are even more expensive for similar offerings. So you're buying your son a system that's at least 2-3 more expensive than Nintendo's gaming system to play kid games that are available on every platform and even phones.... kek what a tard

62360629
>>62348278
>They are getting cooked by real handhelds now and I’m going to be buying another handheld isn’t a switch for my son
Making money out of handheld consoles depends on just 2 factors: How cheap can you make them and how much money can you get from software sales on that console.
Nintendo are the only handheld manufacturer with enough sales volume to get the best manufacturing prices. They are able to even commision custom silicon like Apple does with their phones to optimize things further, something that is completely out of reach of smaller players.
Nintendo also gets a ton of money from game sales and subscriptions as they limit things like the switch internet unless you pay and own game franchises that only release on their hardware.
Meanwhile their competitors need to spend more money to make an equivalent product and the only one that actually get revenue from software sales is Valve.
There's always going to be more powerful handhelds than whatever Nintendo makes as they don't design their stuff for performance, but there's not going to be a single handheld product that is as effective at generating net income as Nintendo's until someone starts getting closer to their sales volume.
Whenever Nintendo doesn't offer the best performance per dollar ratio, it is because they are selling their hardware at a premium due to how strong their brand is, not because they are incapable of making money at a lower pricepoint. Their competitors meanwhile are always running razor thin margins and any major impact to manufacturing costs needs an immediate readjustment to the sales price, as exemplified by the huge price bump the Deck got this month.

TL;DR: Nintendo doesn't make shit deals out of their products because they are incapable of offering something better, but because they have such an advantageous position that they don't need to. If someone ever gets close enough to force them to compete on prices/performance more, they will.

62360701
>>62360531
Millennials who hold good jobs are the most censorious. They were screened by Jewmers for that role. The rest found out recently that supporting that dick sucking networking will not give them high paying jobs if they didn't already have them. That door was already closed on them. With the last seats given out to the worst best friends of nepo baby Millennials they could find.

62360704
There was no normie hype for Switch 2. It was just more of the same. The writing was on the wall.

62361034
There was no normie hype for the iphone 6, or 7, or whatever number. It was more of the same. The writing was on the wall.

62361130
>picking stocks
Why do you retards keep doing this? You can't even have sex and by extension lack any social skills. You don't network. You don't get insider info. All the info and "intelligence" you get about companies and their future performance comes from mainstream sources like Bloomberg and Yahoo finance. You aren't built for trading or even decided where to invest.
Put your arrogance aside for a minute and start buying index funds or your destiny will be complete destitution and bitterness.

You have been warned.

62361211
>>62348170
>If you couldn't tell the vidya industry is dead
I don’t know the statistics behind it, but it sure does feel that way. Same with the movie industry. Nothing is like what it used to be.

62361368
>>62361211
Both the gaming and movie industry are dead. The level of creativity is long gone. Most of the best games that have ever existed are all games that came out early-mid 2000s and before. Most of the gaming that I do today is on the emulator. 8 and 16 bit games are timeless and, unlike 32/64 bit stuff, the graphics age well. It’s also quick to pick up and play, and they are simply fun. Same goes with arcade games. There’s some decent stuff on steam but a lot of creators simply rely on good graphics to carry their games. Besides, there’s only so many ideas that you can come up with for games before it gets old. All the genres have been rehashing the same type of games and all they’ve done is tweak the graphics and stories a bit. It’s unfortunate that newfags will never get to experience how fun the gaming atmosphere of the past used to be like.

62361513
>>62361130
I have made more money doing the opposite of what they tell me half the time. It just takes a bullshit detector skills to figure out if they are lying or not and looking at other company data they are ignoring. Sometimes I have noticed that Blackrock invests like low information investors and then tells normies to pump their bags. In fact just look to see how much mutual funds are already invested into a stock just ready to rug pull. At better strategy is to invest in other things and wait for them to bite. Then you rug pull them. Seriously I won big on investing in mining this way. Nearly everything I invested in they pumped once enough people familiar with the industry took notice. The scary thing is I am a collage dropout who never studied finance. Just self taught in my 30's and a little trial and error. Silver spoon nepo babies can just invest in a little of everything in a sector, hope for the best in a market cycle, and still make money.

62361516
Video gaming has collapsed and recovered multiple times but zoom zooms are too young to remember.

62361524
>>62361130
90% of my money is in boomer mutual funds and I take the remaining 10% and pick stocks. It isn't always one or the other.

62361639
>>62354555
Yeah I don't get people, when I become involved with a company I turn into something of a cheerleader. It's just fun cheering on SK even if I have never heard of them before.

62361715
People can barely afford groceries and you're gambling on bing bing bow companies? Read the room ffs..

62362175
>>62348037
Would have probably made more money on pokemon cards. Anyway dumb as fuck to bet on a company that only knows how remake games about some short fat italian plumber.
